data.title: Impact of Antihypertensive Therapy on Recurrence Risk of Ovarian Cancer for Patients Treated With Bevacizumab Maintenance: A Target Trial Emulation
data.brief_summary: Antiangiogenic therapies like bevacizumab, have notably improved cancer treatment, including for gynecological cancers, by inhibiting the vascular endothelial growth factor and thus limiting tumor growth. In treating advanced ovarian cancer, bevacizumab has been shown to extend progression-free survival by four months, though it also induces or worsens hypertension in 2 to 19% of patients by affecting vascular nitric oxide production or by capillary rarefaction. data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* women with newly ovarian cancer diagnosis (FIGO III to IV)
* age over 18 years old at time of ovarian cancer diagnosis
* diagnosed between January 1, 2011 and December 31, 2020
* with debulking ovarian cancer surgery and adjuvant chemotherapy
